question: Which of the following is a mineral?
description: Tests basic understanding of the different kinds of minerals, and what isn't a mineral.

question: What is the difference between Cleavage and Fracture when identifying minerals?
description: Tests a more moderate understanding of minerals, and specifically what they look like when they are broken.
choices: Cleavage is the tendency to break along round, rugged lines. A fracture is straight, smooth, or regular surfaces.
Cleavage is when minerals crack randomly. A fracture is when lines are less rough and break evenly.
Cleavage is when rocks have a long line down the middle. A fracture is a clean break without rugged surfaces.
Cleavage is the tendency to break along flat, smooth lines. A fracture is irregular, uneven, or curved surfaces.

question: Which of the following explains why diamonds and pencil lead are so different in hardness, even when they are both carbon?
description: Tests a deeper understanding of how minerals are made up and what their structure is.
choices: Their atomic bond strengths are different
Their carbon atom counts are different
The free electrons along the outer shell of diamonds cause diamonds to be more reactive than pencil lead.

question: Which of the following isn't a main type of rock?
description: Tests basic understanding of the different kinds of rocks.

question: How does a sedimentary rock become metamorphic?
description: Tests basic understanding of the way in which rocks are created.

question: What is the "Principle of superposition" in geology?
description: Tests a more moderate understanding of geology principles.
choices: In undisturbed rocks, the youngest is at the bottom and layers above are progressively older
In undisturbed rocks, the oldest is at the bottom and layers above are progressively younger
